sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, Gregory, presents with right ankle pain and swelling after a fall on the ice.
He reports being careful but slipped and fell, and was unable to walk on the ankle initially.
He has been elevating and icing the ankle, taking ibuprofen, which has provided some relief.
He denies numbness in the foot.
He has a history of playing soccer and has had multiple injuries, but is unsure if this is a repeat injury to the same ankle.
Past medical history: [NOT MENTIONED]
Review of systems: [NOT MENTIONED]
Current medications: [NOT MENTIONED]
Objective:
Vital signs: Blood pressure [NOT MENTIONED], pulse rate [NOT MENTIONED], oxygen saturation [NOT MENTIONED], temperature [NOT MENTIONED].
The physical exam reveals ecchymosis and bruising over the lateral malleolus, associated swelling, and tenderness to palpation anterolaterally in the soft tissue.
There is no laxity on anterior drawer and inversion stress, and no bony tenderness on palpation of the foot.
The neurovascular exam is intact, with capillary refill less than three seconds and strong dorsalis pedis pulse.
The right ankle x-ray shows no fracture.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient's symptoms are consistent with a right ankle sprain.
Problem list: Right ankle sprain; status: confirmed.
Plan:
I prescribed ibuprofen as needed for pain management.
I gave the patient an aircast to stabilize the ankle and provided crutches for the next one to two days.
The patient will continue to ice the ankle and elevate it when seated.
The patient will start walking on the ankle as tolerated after one to two days.
Follow-up:
The patient will follow up with me in two weeks to assess progress and determine readiness to return to soccer.
